testSummary: service-local-api 6 files/24 tests pass (new cross-workspace-action.integration 6 tests). Cold full pnpm test 39/39 tasks, 0 cached, no failures. typecheck+lint OK.
e2eSummary: Playwright real Chromium 9/9 incl. strengthened login-workspace-switch: after A->B switch, A-only segment 'Manual VIPs' GONE in-DOM and B-only 'Beta Loyalty Club' appears (new segment list view).
otherGapsFound: sendBroadcast: FIXED — scopedQuery SELECT 1 FROM broadcasts WHERE id=$1 against ctx.workspaceId before runBroadcast; 404 on miss (no existence leak); comment corrected.

addSegmentMembers/removeSegmentMembers/importCsvMembers: FOUND + FIXED — added ownsResource(ctx.workspaceId,'segments',id) guard (404) before mutating manual membership (a WS-A token could otherwise write membership for a WS-B segment id).

Audited ALREADY SAFE: updateSegment/updateCampaign/getProfile use scopedQuery (workspace_id=$1 AND id -> 404 cross-workspace); updateMember explicit WHERE workspace_id=$1; admin handlers intentionally cross-tenant (view_all_workspaces + audited); sending-domain scopes by ctx only (no path id).
commandsRun: cd web && DATABASE_URL=... pnpm exec playwright test
notes: workspace_id always from token ctx never body in every guard. New integration test includes a POSITIVE control (WS-A owner CAN send own broadcast -> 200, 1 outbox in A, 0 in B) so the guard doesn't over-block, plus cross-workspace PUT-campaign/PUT-segment/add-members assertions (WS-B rows unchanged). web SegmentBuilder gained an 'Existing segments' list (GET /segments) reloading on session.workspaceId change -> re-scoping is browser-assertable. SES/SQS/DNS mocked; Postgres never mocked.
